  • Overview
  • Package

  • Tree
  • Index
  • Help

All Classes and Interfaces

Class
Description
Absorber
The Absorber is a stateful Combinator that transfers the identity of one card to another.
Adder
The Adder class is a specific type of Arithmetic modifier that increases a card's numerical value.
AfterMovementEvent
Event fired after a movement phase has been completed in a tick.
Arithmetic
The Arithmetic class is the base for all modifiers that perform mathematical operations on a card's numerical value.
ArithmeticRenderer
Renderer for Arithmetic modifiers.
ArithmeticRenderResolver
Resolves the visual state of an Arithmetic modifier.
AudioManager
The AudioManager serves as the centralized controller for all audio playback.
BackButton
The BackButton is a specialized UI component used for view navigation.
Card
The Card class represents an individual entity within the game grid.
CardCount
The CardCount class is a simple wrapper that associates a Card template with a quantity.
CardEnterEvent
The CardEnterEvent is fired whenever a Card moves into a specific grid coordinate.
CardExitEvent
The CardExitEvent is fired when a Card departs a tile, usually signifying it has reached a goal or terminal point in the path.
CardInputListPane
A UI pane that displays a list of cards available as input for a level.
CardMovement
A record representing a single card's movement from one position to another.
CardMovementAnimation
Handles the visual animation of cards moving between grid positions.
CardOutputListPane
A UI pane that displays a list of cards required for output to complete a level.
CardPaneListItem
A UI component representing a card within a list (e.g., input or output panes).
CardRenderer
Renderer for Card components.
CardRenderResolver
Resolves the visual components of a Card into RenderState objects.
Changer<T>
The Changer class is a generic base for modifiers that alter a specific property of a Card.
Changer.ChangeType
Defines the categories of card properties that can be modified.
CircleEffect
A visual effect that displays a glowing circle on a grid tile.
Combinator
The Combinator class is an abstract base for modifiers that alter the card count within a GameLevel.
CombinatorRenderer
Renderer for Combinator modifiers.
CombinatorRenderResolver
Resolves the visual state of a Combinator modifier.
Config
The Config class centralizes all global constants used throughout the application, including UI sizing, timing, and styling parameters.
Conveyor
The Conveyor is the basic implementation of a Mover.
ConveyorRenderer
Renderer for the Conveyor mover.
ConveyorRenderResolver
Resolves the visual state of a Conveyor mover.
Delay
The Delay is a stateful Mover that stalls card movement.
DelayRenderer
Renderer for the Delay mover.
DelayRenderResolver
Resolves the visual state of a Delay mover.
DeleteOverlay
The DeleteOverlay class provides a visual indicator for tiles marked for deletion.
Direction
The Direction enum represents the cardinal directions and the relative movement within the game grid.
Divider
The Divider class is a specific type of Arithmetic modifier that reduces a card's numerical value using integer division.
Duplicator
The Duplicator class is a stateful Combinator that creates an exact copy of a Card.
EffectManager
The EffectManager class centralizes the creation and management of visual effects within the game world.
EmptyTileRenderer
A renderer for empty game tiles.
EngineEvent
The EngineEvent class represents an event specifically tied to the internal execution cycle of the game engine.
EngineState
The EngineState enum represents the execution states of the TickEngine.
Entrance
The Entrance class is a specialized Pathway that handles the spawning of cards at the start of a level's logic loop.
Event
A base interface for all events within the system.
EventBus
A static event bus that manages event registration and emission.
EventListener<T extends Event>
A functional interface for listening to specific types of events.
Exit
The Exit class is a specialized Pathway that evaluates cards to determine if level objectives have been met.
FlipFlop
The FlipFlop class is a stateful Mover that toggles between two opposing directions.
FlipFlopRenderer
Renderer for the FlipFlop mover.
FlipFlopRenderResolver
Resolves the visual state of a FlipFlop mover.
FloatingLayerRegistry
A registry for managing different rendering layers and their associated JavaFX Panes.
Game
The Game class is the primary orchestrator for the application's lifecycle.
GameBootstrap
The GameBootstrap class handles the registration of all essential game components.
GameEndCondition
Handles checking for level completion conditions.
GameGrid
A GridPane that represents a single layer of the game board.
GameLevel
Represents a game level, including its dimensions, grid, components, and game state.
GameRenderStack
A StackPane that manages all rendering layers of the game grid.
GameState
The GameState enum represents the main phases of a level.
GameTile
The GameTile represents a single cell in the game grid.
GameTilePane
A specialized JavaFX Pane that represents a single tile on the game grid for a specific render layer.
GameView
The main view class for the gameplay screen.
GameWinEvent
Event fired when the game win conditions are met.
GameWinOverlay
An overlay shown when the player wins a level.
GlowOverlay
The GlowOverlay class provides a visual "glow" effect over the entire application screen.
GridIndexable
The GridIndexable interface is implemented by any object that occupies a position on the game grid.
GridPos
The GridPos class represents a 2D coordinate on the game grid.
LevelInfoPane
The LevelInfoPane is the primary sidebar UI component that displays level metadata, player inventory, and game controls.
LevelLoader
The LevelLoader class is responsible for parsing level configuration and layout files from the application resources.
LevelSelectorView
The LevelSelectorView class provides a user interface for browsing and selecting available game levels.
LogicEvent
Base class for all events related to game logic.
Main
The Main class serves as the official entry point for the Cardflow application.
MainMenuView
The MainMenuView class represents the initial landing screen of the application.
Material
The Material enum defines the physical properties and behavioral traits of a Card.
MaterialSetter
The MaterialSetter class is a specialized Setter that changes a Card's physical Material.
Merger
The Merger is a stateful Combinator that adds the value of one card into another, destroying the first in the process.
Modifier
The Modifier class is the abstract foundation for all interactive board components.
ModifyEndedEvent
The ModifyEndedEvent is triggered by the engine once all active modifiers on the board have completed their modify() logic.
MovementEndedEvent
The MovementEndedEvent is emitted by the game engine when all card translations across the grid have concluded for the current tick.
MovementTickResolver
Utility class responsible for resolving card movements during a game tick.
Mover
The Mover class is the abstract foundation for all tiles that control card movement across the grid.
MoverRenderResolver
Abstract base class for mover render resolvers.
MoverRenderResolver.MoverTopology
Utility for resolving the local topology (connections) of a mover on the grid.
MoverRenderResolver.SpriteData
Record holding sprite image and transformation data.
MoverRenderResolver.SpriteSelector
Utility for selecting the correct sprite based on adjacent mover connections.
Multiplier
The Multiplier class is a specific type of Arithmetic modifier that scales a card's numerical value.
ParityFilter
The ParityFilter is a specialized Mover that redirects cards based on their numerical parity.
ParityFilterRenderer
Renderer for the ParityFilter mover.
ParityFilterRenderResolver
Resolves the visual state of a ParityFilter mover.
Pathway
The Pathway class provides the foundation for tiles that track sequential card data, such as Entrance and Exit.
PathwayEvent
The PathwayEvent is the base class for all tile-related occurrences in the game.
PathwayRenderer
Renderer for Pathway modifiers (entrances and exits).
PathwayRenderResolver
Resolves the visual state of a Pathway modifier.
PausedEvent
The PausedEvent is a global signal indicating that the game execution has been suspended.
PlacementController
Singleton controller responsible for managing the logic behind placing, rotating, and dragging tiles/movers within the game grid.
PlacementPathBuilder
Utility class responsible for constructing a path of PlacementPathBuilder.PlacementNodes between two points on the grid.
PlacementPathBuilder.PlacementNode
Represents a single step in a placement path, containing position, direction, and intent (placement vs deletion).
PlacementPathfinder
A utility class that implements the A* pathfinding algorithm tailored for tile placement.
PlacementPathfinder.CostFunction
A functional interface used to determine the cost of moving from one grid cell to another.
PlacementPathfinder.HeuristicFunction
A functional interface used to estimate the remaining distance to the target.
PlacementPathfinder.PathNode
Represents a single point in the calculated path, consisting of a position and the direction the mover is facing at that position.
PlayerInventory
Manages the player's inventory of placeable components (Movers) and handles grid modifications.
RedBlackFilter
The RedBlackFilter is a conditional Mover that sorts cards based on the color associated with their Suit.
RedBlackFilterRenderer
Renderer for the RedBlackFilter mover.
RedBlackFilterRenderResolver
Resolves the visual state of a RedBlackFilter mover.
Renderer<T>
Abstract base class for all renderers in the game.
RendererRegistry
A registry that maps component classes to their corresponding Renderers.
RenderEvent
An event representing a request or notification that specific grid positions need to be re-rendered.
RenderLayer
Enumerates the different layers used in the game's rendering stack.
RenderResolver
Base class for resolvers that load and manage image assets for rendering.
RenderState
A record representing the visual state of a component at a specific moment.
ResetEvent
The ResetEvent is a global command used to restore the game board and engine to their initial level-start conditions.
SelectedTileOverlayRenderer
The SelectedTileOverlayRenderer is a singleton responsible for rendering "ghost" or "preview" versions of tiles being placed by the user.
Setter<T>
The Setter class is an abstract base for all modifiers that set a card property to a specific value.
SetterRenderer
Renderer for Setter modifiers.
SetterRenderResolver
Resolves the visual state of a Setter modifier.
Splitter
The Splitter is a stateful Combinator that divides a card's numerical value and generates a second card from the remainder.
Subtractor
The Subtractor class is an Arithmetic modifier that decreases a card's numerical value.
Suit
Represents the suit of a Card.
SuitSetter
The SuitSetter class is a specialized Setter that changes a Card's Suit.
TickEngine
The TickEngine is the central controller for the game's execution clock.
TickPhase
The TickPhase enum represents the individual steps within a single game logic cycle.
TileSelectChangeEvent
Event fired when the player's selected tile type or rotation changes in the inventory.
Tippable
The Tippable interface should be implemented by any object that can provide a Tooltip for display.
Tooltip
The Tooltip class represents the data model for a UI tooltip.
TooltipLayer
The TooltipLayer is a specialized UI pane that manages the display of dynamic, hierarchical tooltips.
TooltipPane
The TooltipPane is a JavaFX component that displays information provided by a Tooltip.
TransitionType
Defines the available animation styles for switching between Views.
Util
The Util class provides miscellaneous static helper methods for string manipulation and value formatting.
ValueSetter
The ValueSetter class is a specialized Setter that overwrites a Card's numerical value.
Vaporizer
The Vaporizer is a specialized Combinator designed for card removal.
View
The View class serves as the abstract base for all visual screens in the application.
ViewManager
The ViewManager coordinates all navigation and visual transitions within the application.
